channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Massive Turnout In Edo LG Polls As Okpebholo Hails Exercise
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A large number of voters in Edo State came out yesterday to cast their votes to elect chairmen in the 18 local government areas and 192 ward councilors. The exercise was adjudged to be peaceful as eligible voters were seen exercising their franchise in most of the wards visited. In Esan Central loc

item.title: UN envoy, Ibrahim, seeks global debt-for-education swap at Paris UNESCO Conference
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Nigeria’s Permanent Representative to the United Nations, Ambassador Jimoh Ibrahim, has called for a bold global debt-for-education swap initiative to enable developing countries to invest more in education without undermining their debt obligations.  According to a statement issued by hi

item.title: World Cup 2026: Players with most goal contributions [FULL LIST]
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Ahead of the 2026 World Cup semi-final fixtures taking place on Tuesday and Wednesday, DAILY POST brings you the full list of players with the most combined goals and assists at the tournament. France captain, Kylian Mbappe, currently tops the list with 11goal contributions. The Real Madrid forward

item.title: PDP Faction Uploads Presidential, NASS Candidates To INEC Portal
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: The faction of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) loyal to the Minister of the Federal Capital Territory (FCT), Nyesom Wike has uploaded its candidates for the presidential and National Assembly elections to the Independent National Electoral Commission (INEC) portal. The announcement was made on Su

item.title: ICPC Seeks Stronger Collaboration To Deepen Anti-corruption Fight
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Chairman of the Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC), Musa Adamu Aliyu, has called for greater collaboration among governments, anti-corruption agencies, civil society organisations, the private sector, the media and citizens to strengthen integrity and scale up

item.title: South Africa Mourns World Cup Midfielder Jayden Adams Following Tragic Death At 25
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: South African football has been plunged into mourning following the death of Bafana Bafana and Mamelodi Sundowns midfielder Jayden Adams. He was 25. The South African Football Players Union announced Adams’ passing on Saturday, July 11, describing his death as a devastating loss after he proudly rep

item.title: World Cup 2026: Erling Haaland makes promise after England eliminate Norway
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Norway striker, Erling Haaland, has vowed to keep working hard, improve himself and do everything he can to help his team achieve better results going forward. Haaland spoke at his post-match interview after England eliminated Norway from the World Cup in a quarter-final encounter on Saturday. Recal

item.title: Troops Intercept Truck Loaded With Illicit Drugs On Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army‘s 65 Battalion have intercepted a truck conveying a large consignment of illicit drugs along the Lagos-Calabar Coastal Highway following actionable intelligence from a credible source. According to a statement by the Acting Deputy Director, Army Public Relations, 81

item.title: Atiku criticises proposed ₦50,000 WAEC, NECO examination fees
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Former Vice President and African Democratic Congress (ADC) presidential candidate, Atiku Abubakar, has criticised the Federal Government over the proposed ₦50,000 examination fee for candidates sitting WAEC and NECO examinations from 2027.

item.title: State Police: Tinubu’s Initiative A Masterstroke Against Insecurity — Barau
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The Deputy President of the Senate, Senator Barau Jibrin, has described President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s push for the establishment of state police as a “masterstroke” aimed at tackling insecurity and strengthening Nigeria’s security architecture. Barau, who also chairs the Senate Committee on C

item.title: 2027: INEC portal becomes first test of parties’ readiness
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: …PDP confirms upload of presidential, NASS candidates as INEC extends nomination deadline to July 14 …ADC, PRP report smooth exercise …Other parties battle procedural hurdles By Luminous Jannamike ABUJA — The Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) says it has successfully uploaded the names of its president

item.title: Conference 57 CPS Honours Akosile For Championing Strategic Communication
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Special Adviser on Media and Publicity to the Lagos State Governor, Mr. Gboyega Akosile, has been honoured with an Award of Excellence by the Conference 57 Chief Press Secretaries (CPS) for his contributions to strategic communication and public information management. Conference 57 CPS is the umbre

item.title: With our population we can determine who governs us – Bishop Oyedepo to church members
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Bishop David Oyedepo of the Living Faith Church has said the church will have a say in the coming election, citing the numerical strength of the organization. Oyedepo, who spoke on Sunday while delivering a sermon titled, ‘Breaking the Curses of Life’, during a service at Canaanland, Ota

item.title: Peller, Jarvis announce wedding date
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: Habeeb Hamzat, popularly known as Peller and his partner, Amadou Elizabeth Aminata, known as Jarvis, have announced readiness to get married... The post Peller, Jarvis announce wedding date appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Bello Turji, Ado Aleru should be eliminated like Kachalla – Shehu Sani
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Former Kaduna Central Senator, Shehu Sani, has disclosed that bandit leaders, Bello Turji and Ado Aleru, should be eliminated the same way Zamfara bandit leader, Yellow Kachalla was killed. Sani said the elimination of Kachalla is a major victory in the fight against terrorism in Northern Nigeria. P

item.title: US senator and Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies aged 71
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Senator Lindsey Graham, an influential ally of President Donald Trump and advocate of hawkish US foreign policy, has died unexpectedly aged 71, his office said on Sunday. Graham was a staunch supporter of the Iran war, and in recent years urged both Trump and the Biden administrations to back K

item.title: Oriire Abduction: Rescued Principal’s Family Thanks Tinubu, Makinde, Security Personnel
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The family of Professor Wole Alamu has expressed deep appreciation to Almighty God and to all Nigerians, both at home and abroad, for their prayers, moral and financial support, and collective efforts that led to the safe rescue of their loved ones from the captivity of bandits. In a statement signe

item.title: Leave Rescued Pupils Out of Dirty Politics — Femi Branch Faults Governor Makinde’s Remark
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Nigerian actor and filmmaker Femi Branch has called on public office holders to refrain from politicising the recent rescue of abducted pupils and teachers in Oyo State.   The hostages were freed on Friday following a joint operation by the military, police, and Department of State Services (DS

item.title: Troops neutralise two terrorists in Zamfara, recover motorcycles
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army have neutralised two suspected terrorists and recovered two motorcycles during a fighting patrol in Maradun Local Government Area of Zamfara State. The development was disclosed in a report by Zagazola Makama, a counter-insurgency and security analyst covering the Lake Ch

item.title: Experts Seek Broader Reforms For Better Welfare
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Two public policy experts have urged the Federal Government to adopt broader economic reforms beyond periodic increases in the national minimum wage, warning that salary reviews alone cannot improve the living conditions of most Nigerians. In a policy paper titled, “Beyond Minimum Wage Increases: To

item.title: Stop turning markets into dumpsites or have them shut – Ogun Govt warns traders
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Ogun State Government, through the Ogun Waste Management Authority, OGWAMA, has warned traders to stop depositing waste inside markets, stressing that it later turn to dumpsites and constitutes environmental and health hazards. The Managing Director of OGWAMA, Farouk Akintunde, noted that if the

item.title: 2027: Oyedepo Urges Worshippers To Secure PVCs
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Bishop David Oyedepo, founder of the Living Faith Church, on Sunday urged worshippers to secure their Permanent Voter Cards (PVCs) ahead of the country’s 2027 general elections. He gave the advice while delivering a sermon at Canaan Land, in Ota, Ogun, where the congregation gathered for a service t

item.title: Federal Government Condoles Qatar Over Death Of Former Emir Sheikh Hamad
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: The federal government has expressed deep sorrow over the death of the Father Amir and former Emir of the State of Qatar, Sheikh Hamad bin Khalifa Al Thani, describing him as a visionary leader whose legacy transformed Qatar into a modern global power. Sheikh Hamad, who ruled Qatar from 1995 to 2013

item.title: US insists Strait of Hormuz is open as it exchanges strikes with Iran
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The strikes follow an attack on a Cyprus-flagged vessel travelling through the critical waterway.

item.title: US senator and close Trump ally Lindsey Graham dies after 'brief and sudden illness'
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Donald Trump paid tribute to the 71-year-old South Carolina senator, calling him a "true American patriot" who will be "greatly missed".

item.description: In what appears to be a coordinated push to elevate Nigeria’s standing in the global financial ecosystem, a high-powered delegation read more Kwairanga leads high-level NGX, CSCS delegation to meet FTSE Russell in London

item.title: APC Sweeps All Seats in Edo LG Election
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: The All Progressives Congress (APC) has won all the 18 chairmanship seats and the 192 councillorship positions contested in Saturday’s local government election in Edo State. Announcing the results on Sunday, Chairman of the Edo State Independent Electoral Commission (EDSIEC), Jonathan Aifuobhokhan,

item.title: As five big U.S. banks report earnings on the same day, Citigroup is the one to watch
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Among the largest U.S. banks, Citigroup is expected to show the greatest improvement by one important measure. But it still has a long way to go to reach its own performance target.

item.title: Jide Ekene Explains Why Yoruba Filmmakers Are Dominating Nollywood
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Prominent Nigerian actor and filmmaker Jide Ekene has shared his thoughts on the growing dominance of the Yoruba film industry, crediting its success to stronger investments, compelling storytelling and unwavering support for indigenous language productions. Speaking during an interview with Tochi,

item.title: Big Banks Set Up Strong Earnings Week
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg Intelligence analyst Herman Chan says major US banks are poised to report strong earnings, driven by trading, investment banking and resilient lending, with investors focused on consumer health, credit quality, net interest margins and AI-related financing activity. item.title: Chiwetalu Agu warns singles against overemphasising sexual history
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Agu emphasised the need for mutual understanding before tying the knot, stating that respect and kindness are what builds long lasting relationships.

item.title: Borno youths, students demand rescue of abducted pupils
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: A coalition of youths and students on Sunday staged a protest in Maiduguri, the Borno State capital, against insecurity and other forms of criminality in the North-East and across Nigeria. The protesters, carrying placards with various inscriptions, called on the Federal Government to deploy all ava

item.title: Alledged wrong parking: Why Peter Obi may not apologise, pay N25,000 airport fine- Udim
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Dickson Omobola Senior Partner at Justice Chambers, Ekemini Udim, has identified reasons why the presidential candidate of Nigeria Democratic Congress, NDC, Mr Peter Obi, may not apologise to airport officials and pay a N25,000 fine over an alleged parking violation at the Nnamdi Azikiwe Airport,

item.title: Iran newspaper lists Trump, European leaders as revenge targets
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: A conservative newspaper in Iran known for its provocative tone has published a list of people to be targeted as revenge for the killing of supreme leader Ali Khamenei, including the leaders of the US, Israel and European countries. US-Israeli strikes killed the late ayatollah on February 28, the fi

item.title: Don Seeks Gender-Inclusive Policies To Address Climate Displacement
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: A national security and governance expert at the School of Oriental and African Studies (SOAS), University of London, Dr Folahanmi Aina, has called for integrated and gender-responsive policies to address the growing challenge of climate-induced displacement and insecurity across northern Nigeria. A

item.title: Heidi Klum Praises Ex-Husband Seal’s Strength In Rare Comment On His Lupus Journey
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Supermodel Heidi Klum has made a rare public comment about her ex-husband, singer Seal, praising his resilience as he continues to inspire many with his journey living with lupus. Speaking about the award-winning musician, Klum reflected on how Seal has handled the autoimmune disease with remarkable

item.title: World Cup 2026: England Have An Uphill Task Against Argentina –Ex-Man City Star
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Former Manchester City defender Micah Richards has disclosed that England have a mountain to climb against Argentina in the semi-final of the FIFA World Cup 2026. The Three Lions reached this stage by defeating Norway in the quarter-finals, while Argentina advanced with a gritty 3-1 extra-time victo

item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: David Willey, esteemed BBC foreign correspondent, dies aged 93
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Willey is best known for covering the Vatican, and reported on five popes during his long career.

item.title: France temporarily shuts down three nuclear reactors over heatwave
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: France's main energy provider on Sunday said that three nuclear reactors have been temporarily shut down, while eight others are operating at reduced power. The measure is an environmental protection requirement to avoid discharging too much hot water into rivers already warming from the heatwave.

item.title: Reps don’t need Tinubu’s presence to resolve budget issues — Agbese
item.dc:creator: Tribune

item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Folarin Kehinde The National Population Commission (NPC) has stressed the importance of a credible, technology-driven Population and Housing Census, saying Nigeria cannot effectively plan or allocate resources without accurate population data. The Chairman of the Commission, Dr Aminu Yusuf, state

item.title: Tinubu’s State Police Proposal ‘Masterstroke’ Against Insecurity – Jibrin
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Deputy President of the Senate and Chairman of the Senate Committee on the Review of the 1999 Constitution, Senator Barau Jibrin, has described President Bola Ahmed Tinubu’s push for state police as a “masterstroke” to decisively tackle insecurity nationwide. item.description: The Federal Road Safety Corps, FRSC, Abia State Sector Command, says it would commence the 2026 road transport safety standardization scheme, RTSSS, enforcement week in the state on Monday, saying that it is a strategic effort at reducing road traffic fatalities and injuries. The Sector Commander of

item.title: Umahi fires back at Obi over rejected governance debate
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Minister of Works David Umahi has responded to Peter Obi's rejection of his invitation to a public debate, insisting the proposed engagement was about comparing governance records and not political status. The post Umahi fires back at Obi over rejected governance debate appeared first on The Sun Nig

item.title: 107,180 pupils write common entrance examination in Jigawa
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The Jigawa State Universal Basic Education Board (SUBEB) says no fewer than 107,180 primary school pupils sat for the 2026 common entrance examination into junior secondary schools across the state. The Executive Chairman of SUBEB, Professor Haruna Musa, disclosed this while inspecting examination c

item.title: Israeli forces kill five Palestinians in latest attacks on Gaza
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Since a US-brokered ceasefire in October, Israeli attacks have killed at least 1,098 Palestinians in Gaza.

item.title: Police arrest 43 suspects in Ogun raid
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Babaseyi said that the July 8 operation by the Special Weapons and Tactial Unit (SWAT) followed credible intelligence linking the location opposite Kara Cattle Market to cultism, drug abuse, prostitution and other criminal activities.

item.title: Ex-Flying Eagles boss takes charge at Sudan’s Al Ahli Madani
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Sudanese club Al Ahli Madani have appointed Aliyu Zubairu as their new head coach on a two-year contract, DAILY POST reports. Zubairu, a former Flying Eagles head coach, previously managed Egyptian second division outfit, Telecom SC. The 62-year-old’s contract was mutually terminated by Teleco

item.title: World Cup 2026: Former Spain Prime Minster Criticised For ‘Racist’ Comments About France Team
item.dc:creator: Completesports
item.description: Spain’s former Prime Minister Mariano Rajoy has been accused of racism by several leading French politicians after saying the France national team “does not have any French players.” Rajoy, who served as the leader of the country’s government between 2011 and 2018, made the comment in a column for S

item.title: Benue, Plateau communities hit by fresh attacks, 27 killed
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Peter Duru & Golok Nanmwa No fewer than 27 persons have been killed in separate attacks on communities in Benue and Plateau states, following fresh assaults by suspected armed groups that have left residents mourning and demanding urgent government intervention. In Benue State, at least 18 pe

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Senegal sack Thiaw as manager
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Pape Thiaw has been sacked as Senegal’s head coach, following their exit from the 2026 World Cup. The Teranga Lions were eliminated by Belgium in the round of 32. They had lost their opening two Group I fixtures to France and Norway, but a 5-0 thrashing of Iran saw the West Africans qualify as

item.title: What made US Republican Senator Lindsey Graham a controversial figure
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Graham was known for his hawkish views on US defence and foreign policies.

item.title: Police confirm killing of notorious Zamfara bandit, Sani Yellow
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The Zamfara State Police Command has confirmed the killing of notorious bandit commander Kachalla Sani Yellow in Anka Local Government Area, but says it did not carry out the operation, leaving conflicting accounts over the circumstances surrounding his death unresolved. item.description: A notorious bandit commander, identified as Kachalla Sani Yellow, has been killed in Zamfara State by local hunters and members of the Civilian Joint Task Force. The bandit kingpin was reportedly killed in Anka Local Government Area of the state after a gun battle between the vigilantes and a group

item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: On Tuesday, a US Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer fatally shot Salgado Araujo, 52, after he was pursued by federal agents driving unmarked vehicles while he was taking his crew to their latest job site. item.title: Lindsey Graham dies suddenly, aged 71: "It was quite a shock"
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Sen. Lindsey Graham, one of President Donald Trump's closest allies in Congress who traveled the globe to advocate for a more aggressive U.S. foreign policy, has died after a “brief and sudden illness,” his office said. He was 71. Our correspondent Fraser Jackson tells us more.

item.title: SERAP sues INEC over alleged diversion of N800bn by APC governors for 2027 campaign
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: SERAP asks a Federal High Court to compel INEC to investigate allegations of an N800bn campaign fund linked to APC governors. The post SERAP sues INEC over alleged diversion of N800bn by APC governors for 2027 campaign appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu pays tribute to Lindsey Graham, "a great friend of Israel"
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description:
Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu called Graham a "great friend of Israel."
"In our recent meeting, I said, 'Lindsey is a great friend of Israel and a cherished friend of mine. We have no better friend than Lindsey.' Lindsey understood that the security of Israel and America are inseparabl

item.title: Phoebe Gates Startup Draws Tracking Scrutiny
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Bloomberg technology editor Olivia Solon says Phoebe Gates' shopping startup, Phia, appears to overwrite affiliate tracking cookies, potentially redirecting sales commissions from publishers and influencers without users intentionally engaging with the service. She says Phia has acknowledged and fix

item.title: UniAbuja promotes 34 academics to professorial ranks
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Joseph Erunke, Abuja The Governing Council of the University of Abuja, now Yakubu Gowon University, has approved the promotion of 34 academic staff to professorial ranks. The approval was granted under the leadership of the Council Chairman, Distinguished Senator Dr Olanrewaju Tejuoso. Seventeen

item.title: Calabar flood: Parents mourn as landslide kills four children
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A rainfall-induced landslide killed four siblings and one adult in Calabar, prompting calls for a geotechnical assessment of the affected area. The post Calabar flood: Parents mourn as landslide kills four children appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Bellingham scores twice to lift England past Haaland and Norway 2-1
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Bellingham scored twice on Saturday — an equaliser in the first half and the go-ahead goal in the third minute of extra time — to lift England past Norway 2-1 and into the World Cup semi-finals for the first time since 2018. We discuss the match with our guest Robbie Nock, lead commentator and journ

item.title: NiMet Records New Milestone As FG Approves Reviewed Scheme Of Service
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Federal Government has approved the Reviewed Scheme of Service for the Nigerian Meteorological Agency (NiMet), marking a landmark and progressive move. The move, it was learnt, would further amplify momentum to the ongoing rebirth and secure fair wages, with the overall aim of empowering Nigeria

item.title: Army intercepts truck conveying illicit drugs along Lagos–Calabar coastal road
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Troops of the Nigerian Army's 65 Battalion intercepted a truck carrying illicit drugs and handed the suspect and exhibits to the NDLEA in Lagos. The post Army intercepts truck conveying illicit drugs along Lagos–Calabar coastal road appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Edo LG poll: APC wins all 18 chairmanship, 192 councillorship positions
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: EDSIEC has declared the APC winner of all 18 chairmanship and 192 councillorship seats in Edo State's local government election. The post Edo LG poll: APC wins all 18 chairmanship, 192 councillorship positions appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: NELFUND Debunks Fake Notice On Suspension Of Students Upkeep Allowances
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: The Nigerian Education Loan Fund (NELFUND) has debunked a social media document claiming that the payment of upkeep allowances under the Federal Government’s Student Loan Scheme for the 2025/2026 academic session has been suspended. The post NELFUND Debunks Fake Notice On Suspension Of Students Upke

item.title: Nabors Industries Ltd. (NBR) Is A Top Stock To Buy According To Bill Miller
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Crescent Energy Company (CRGY) Is A Top Stock In Miller Value Partners’ Filings
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Gray Media, Inc. (GTN) Is A Top Stock To Buy According To Miller Value Partners
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance
